ownership.name: Mike Brown
ownership.style: Notoriously frugal; family-run operation; historically reluctant to spend in free agency
ownership.tendencies: Loyal to coaches despite losing — retained Zac Taylor after 6-11 season and three straight years without playoffs; no traditional GM title in organization

gm.name: Duke Tobin (Director of Player Personnel — de facto GM)
gm.philosophy: Draft-and-develop; conservative free agency spending; relies heavily on scouting department
gm.analytics_approach: Traditional scouting emphasis; one of the less analytics-driven front offices

head_coach.name: Zac Taylor
head_coach.scheme_offense: Spread passing attack built around Joe Burrow; three-wide sets; Sean McVay tree concepts
head_coach.scheme_defense: 4-3 base; zone coverage; needs significant talent infusion across all levels

decision_makers.primary: Mike Brown (owner) has final say; Duke Tobin (personnel) leads scouting and draft board

roster_2026.qb_situation: Joe Burrow — locked in as franchise QB; elite talent but team around him has deteriorated
key_departures: Trey Hendrickson (EDGE, signed with Ravens for $112M/4yr)

needs.reason: Pressing need; in prime position to address at #10 with Mansoor Delane (LSU) or Jermod McCoy (Tennessee) likely available; Round 1 priority after signing Mafe at EDGE
Defense needs help at every level; linebacker corps is thin
Defensive line needs reinforcement despite Jonathan Allen signing; overall defensive talent significantly lacking
